pins.notes: Resolver Sine winding -
Resolver Sine winding +
Resolver Cosine winding -
Resolver Cosine winding +
Resolver excitation output
Resolver excitation return
Resolver Shield Ground - **DO NOT connect to 12V system ground or motor case.**
Encoder Channel A input - Refer to Appendix D for analog encoder and hall sensor options.
Encoder Channel B input - Refer to Appendix D for analog encoder and hall sensor options.
Encoder Channel Z input (Index) - Refer to Appendix D for analog encoder and hall sensor options.
High Side Driver - If precharge function is used this output serves as the precharge contactor coil driving output
12V/24V Constant Voltage - Intended for constant 12V power, when in the off state input current is less than 1mA
12V/24V Constant Voltage - Intended for constant 12V power, when in the off state input current is less than 1mA
CAN Channel A High CAN high Port A
CAN Channel A Low CAN low Port A
CAN A Terminator Loop - Short to CANA_L to include a 120 ohm terminator from CANA_H to CANA_L.
CAN ground - CAN is isolated from GND/KL31. CAN shield should be connected here. DO NOT connect to 12V system ground.
CAN B Terminator Loop - Short to CANB_L to include a 120 ohm terminator from CANB_H to CANB_L.
CAN Channel B High
CAN Channel B Low
Program Enable - If connected to RS232_GND at power up inverter will go into bootloader mode for use with C2prog software. DO NOT connect to 12V system ground.
Digital Ground - Ground return for encoder signals.
High Side Driver - If the precharge function is used this output serves as the main contactor output.
Switched 12V/24V - Turns on internal power supplies to power up the inverter and start communications.
RTD1 Positive - Allows connection of PT100 or PT1000 RTD for temperature monitoring.
RTD1 Negative - See RTD1P
RTD2 Positive - Allows connection of PT100 or PT1000 RTD for temperature monitoring.
RTD2 Negative - See RTD2P
Digital Input 1 - STG Forward Enable Switch (if used)
Digital Input 2 - STG Reverse Enable Switch (if used)
Digital Input 3 - STG Brake Switch (if used)
Digital Input 4 - STG Digital Input 4 - STG
High Voltage Interlock Input When all high voltage plugs are installed HVIL_IN will be connected to HVIL_OUT.
5V power for transducers - Used with sensors connected to analog inputs.
Lo-Side Relay Driver - OK Indicator Drive / 12V Power Relay Coil Drive
Safety Switched 12V/24V - If voltage is removed then inverter will declare a hardware gate fault, a completely hardware based disable of the PWM from the inverter.
Analog Ground - For use with analog inputs. **DO NOT connect to 12V system ground.**
Analog Input 1
Analog Input 2
Analog Input 3
Analog Input 4
RS232 Ground - Normally connected to pin 5 of DB9 connector. **DO NOT connect to 12V system ground.**
RS232 Inverter Receive - Normally connected to pin 3 of DB9 connector.
RS232 Inverter Transmit - Normally connected to pin 2 of DB9.
High Voltage Interlock Output - See HVIL_IN
Analog Ground - For use with analog inputs. **DO NOT connect to 12V system ground.**
Lo-Side Relay Driver Fault Indicator Coil Drive
12V/24V System Ground - 12V/24V system return.
